input.command: docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.ID}}" | tail -n +2 | while read name id; do echo "=== Container: $name ($id) ==="; docker inspect "$id" | grep -A 20 '"Mounts"' | head -30; echo ""; done

input.command: which docker && docker version 2>&1 || echo "Docker not available or permission denied"

tool_attempts.result_summary: Docker not available or permission denied

final_response:
**Summary:**

Docker is not available in this sandboxed environment. However, if Docker were available, here's the command I would use to show all volume mounts for running containers:

```bash
docker ps --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.ID}}" | tail -n +2 | while read name id; do 
  echo "=== Container: $name ($id) ===" 
  docker inspect "$id" --format '{{json .Mounts}}' | jq '.'
  echo ""
done
```

Or a simpler one-liner using jq:

```bash
docker inspect $(docker ps -q) --format '{{.Name}}{{.Moun
